study: Sangram SG-MO-025 (Инфинитив) — native positive; diachronic -tum reduction
kind: beyond-quota core ① (opening set already 19/19); native positive (VerbForm=Inf)

snapshot.provenance_note: pin 04e0778 orphaned; binding = provenance table + SHA-256 + tag c3-pin-04e0778-content

infinitive_native_positive.diachronic_note: classical Sanskrit reduced the infinitive to the accusative -tum alone; the corpus preserves the Vedic plethora (frozen case-forms of a verbal noun): -tave/-tavai (dat.), -toḥ (gen./abl.), -dhyai. The -tum share (~82%) vs Vedic remnant (~5%) shows the reduction.
infinitive_native_positive.case_note: feat_case=None; the frozen case-nature of the Vedic infinitives is the traditional analysis (Whitney §§968-988), recovered by surface morphology, not a DCS annotation

cross_reference.note: completes the non-finite deverbal quartet: gerundive (SG-MO-024, Gdv), absolutive (SG-MO-026, Conv); the infinitive shares the -tavya/-tum base with the gerundive

traditional_layer.witness: Whitney 1889 §§968-988 (infinitive: accusative -tum + the Vedic case-form infinitives)

limits.suffix_by_surface: the suffix/case split is by surface morphology within the clean Inf set; DCS does not tag case (feat_case=None); 'other' ~ -tum sandhi variants
limits.vedic_bias: the Vedic remnant share reflects the pinned corpus's Vedic content; not a claim about any single register
limits.pin: orphaned 04e0778, bound by provenance table + SHA-256 + tag
